_schema.description: Locations referenced in the research. Names captured for the era (1900s-1940s) and modern equivalents.
_schema.era_notes: These were Austro-Hungarian Empire (until 1918), then Second Polish Republic (1918-1939), then variously USSR/Generalgouvernement/USSR/independent (Ukraine/Poland) afterward.

era_context.1911: Kingdom of Galicia and Lodomeria, Austria-Hungary; district seat south of Stanisławów at the foot of the Carpathians
era_context.1941_1944: Distrikt Galizien, German Generalgouvernement — ghetto established

places.significance: BIRTHPLACE of David Rapaport (1911) and Rebeka Griffel (1888). The Griffel-Chajes families were merchants here. Site of the ghetto David escaped from.
places.significance_he: מקום הולדתו של דוד רפפורט (1911) ושל רבקה גריפל (1888). משפחות גריפל-חיות היו סוחרים במקום. אתר הגטו שממנו ברח דוד.
places.significance_pl: Miejsce urodzenia Dawida Rapaporta (1911) i Rebeki Griffel (1888). Rodziny Griffel-Chajes były tu kupcami. Miejsce getta, z którego Dawid uciekł.
places.significance_fr: Lieu de naissance de David Rapaport (1911) et de Rebeka Griffel (1888). Les familles Griffel-Chajes y étaient commerçantes. Site du ghetto d'où David s'est échappé.

era_context.1913: Kingdom of Galicia, Austria-Hungary; town south of Stryj. Home to the 'Lower Imperial-Royal Forestry School' (Niższa Cesarsko-Królewska Szkoła Lasowa) from 1883 — became Państwowa Szkoła dla Leśniczych in 1922.
era_context.1939_: Soviet, then German occupation, then Soviet, now Ukraine

places.significance: BIRTHPLACE of Leah Weitzner (1913) and her siblings Feige (1911), Moses (1916). The Weitzner family — Elias (cattle dealer / tanner) and Matel (née Weinreb) — lived in Bolechów Ruski (a suburb, incorporated into the city in 1929). Grandmother of the family started here.

era_context.1905: District seat in Austrian Galicia; the administrative seat for the area including Bolechów
era_context.1930: Had multiple sawmills: Glessinger I. Ph. Sp. Akc., Popadnik J. (steam-driven); Jekel I Jungermann, Jolles I.H., Popper H. (other drive)

places.significance: Origin of the Weinreb family. Samuel Weinreb (Matel's father) was a tavern keeper here. Matel and Elias Weitzner married here in 1905. ALSO: one of the leading hypotheses for what 'Mosina' in family memory might actually be — Basia notes 'Dolina' has the same letter count and same ending as 'Mosina', a possible orthographic mishearing.

era_context.1918_1939: Lwów, capital of Lwów Voivodeship, Poland
era_context.1944_: Soviet, then Ukrainian SSR, now Lviv, Ukraine. Yalta Conference (Feb 1945) confirmed Lviv would remain in USSR.

places.significance: David Rapaport's residence as of 1 Jan 1938 (per Brussels DP card). Where David and Lusia survived the war under false identities. Lusia lived at Legionów Street 24 (today's Prospekt Svobody 24) as 'Maria Cizlik' — a four-storey Biedermeier kamienica built 1836-1837 by architect Johann Salzmann for F. Adamski, where Polish national poet Wincenty Pol lived 1856-1866. The street was renamed Adolf-Hitler-Ring during the 1941-1944 Nazi occupation. David worked on rail/road construction. David's sister Lota lived in Lwów too.
places.significance_he: מקום מגוריו של דוד רפפורט נכון ל-1 בינואר 1938 (לפי כרטיס ה-DP מבריסל). שם שרדו דוד ולוסיה את המלחמה תחת זהויות בדויות. לוסיה התגוררה ברחוב לגיונוב 24 (היום פרוספקט סבובודי 24) בזהות 'מריה ציצליק' — בניין אבן ביידרמייר בן ארבע קומות שנבנה ב-1836-1837 על-ידי האדריכל יוהן זלצמן עבור פ. אדמסקי, ובו גר המשורר הפולסי וינצנטי פול 1856-1866. הרחוב נקרא אדולף-היטלר-רינג בזמן הכיבוש הנאצי 1941-1944. דוד עבד בבניית כבישים ומסילות. אחותו של דוד לוטה התגוררה אף היא בלבוב.
places.significance_pl: Miejsce zamieszkania Dawida Rapaporta od 1 stycznia 1938 (wg brukselskiej karty DP). Tu Dawid i Lusia przetrwali wojnę pod fałszywymi tożsamościami. Lusia mieszkała przy ulicy Legionów 24 (dzisiejszy Prospekt Swobody 24) jako 'Maria Cizlik' — czteropiętrowa kamienica w stylu biedermeier zbudowana w latach 1836-1837 przez Johanna Salzmanna dla F. Adamskiego. Polski poeta narodowy Wincenty Pol mieszkał tam w latach 1856-1866. Podczas okupacji nazistowskiej 1941-1944 ulica nosiła nazwę Adolf-Hitler-Ring.
places.significance_fr: Lieu de résidence de David Rapaport au 1er janvier 1938 (selon la fiche DP de Bruxelles). David et Lusia y survécurent à la guerre sous de fausses identités. Lusia habitait rue Legionów 24 (aujourd'hui Prospekt Svobody 24) sous l'identité « Maria Cizlik » — un immeuble Biedermeier de quatre étages construit en 1836-1837 par l'architecte Johann Salzmann pour F. Adamski, où le poète national polonais Wincenty Pol vécut de 1856 à 1866. La rue fut rebaptisée Adolf-Hitler-Ring durant l'occupation nazie de 1941-1944.

era_context.1939_09: After 17 Sep 1939 Soviet invasion: Przemyśl was DIVIDED by the German-Soviet border along the San River — German side vs Soviet side

places.significance: Leah fled here in 1939 (early war). Worked as teller / passport section under Soviets. Visited David's parents here. The exact people she stayed with (Matel? Regina/Rebeka?) is an open question — see hypotheses.
Where the family arrived after the war. David registered as Displaced Person on 9 April 1946. Dov Bernard born here in 1946. The 'B.' on the DP card stands for Brussels or Belgium.
Last stop in Poland before Brussels (per family memory). Family came here from Lwów; from here travelled by bus to Brussels.

Resort town in southern Poland. Rebeka/Regina Rapaport (David's mother) registered at 'Alfredówka' here on 25 July 1912 as wife of a Nadwórna merchant — confirms her identity in Polish press of the era.

Listed on David's 1946 Brussels DP card as his birthplace (Tesin/Cieszyn). SUPERSEDED by the Nadwórna birth certificate (15 May 2026 finding). Likely a French clerk's mishearing of 'Nadwórna' or some other transcription error. The notation looked like a French cursive uppercase letter; team considered T, H, C as possibilities.

Sawmill owned by 'Rapaport S. i Liptan M.' listed in the 1930 Polish trade directory — northwest of Lwów, near today's Polish border between Niemirów and Jawor. Connection to the family unverified. May be unrelated.

The town where, per Leah's memoir, David managed a family-owned sawmill, where they married, and where Shimon was born (1937). They lived there until 1939. BUT: the only 'Mosina' in 1933 Poland was near Poznań (Greater Poland), where genealogy shows no Jewish Rapaports and no family-owned sawmill (the two Mosina sawmills in 1930 were owned by Władysław Litke and Władysław Urbaniak — Polish, non-Jewish names). Candidates being investigated: Morszyn (resort near Stryj), Muszyna (resort near Krynica), Mszana Dolna, Dolina (sound-alike with similar ending). See hypotheses.

candidates.evidence_for: Magda's recollection of a past conversation with Dov Rapaport
Dana Rapaport's note about 'Rapaports of Mszana' as forest people; family found tagline 'Rapaport Sawmill' or 'The Masina'
candidates.evidence_against: Not in 1930 industry directory for Mszana
Doesn't match phonetically when spoken; sawmills there are not Rapaport-owned

places.significance: Spa resort town near Stryj. Kasia of the Jewish Historical Institute (ŻIH) hypothesized this as a candidate for the 'Mosina' in family memory.
Spa resort town near Krynica in southern Poland. Magda recalled discussing this area in a previous conversation with Dov Rapaport. Candidate for 'Mosina'.

Town in Kraków Voivodeship. Family research note (Dana, 14 May) mentions 'Rapaports of Mszana' as 'forest people' (ludzie lasu) involved in timber trade. Not in 1930 industry directory but may be relevant pre-1930.

Only 'Mosina' in 1933 Polish placenames index. Greater Poland. Jewish population peaked at 206 in 1871, declined to 0 by 1930s. The 2 sawmills in 1930 were owned by Władysław Litke and Władysław Urbaniak — non-Jewish Polish names. Birth records 1911 do not include David Rapaport.

era_context.1850: Kingdom of Galicia and Lodomeria, Austria-Hungary — birthplace of Sarah Matel Chajes

places.note_en: Birthplace of Sarah Matel Chajes (Eliezer Griffel's wife), connecting the family to the Chajes rabbinical dynasty of Kolomea.
Birthplace of cousin Edward Gelles (1927) — published genealogist of the Griffel-Nadwórna dynasty. Also where the Griffel timber business reached commercial Vienna.

era_context.1945_now: Post-war home of several Griffel-Nadwórna descendants (Yehuda Nir d.2014, Steven Lamm, Andrew Griffel)

places.note_en: Several Griffel-line cousins of David Memek Rapaport settled in NYC: Yehuda Nir (1930-2014, author of 'The Lost Childhood'); Dr Steven Lamm (b.1948, NYU Langone); Andrew Griffel (attorney, splits time with Israel).
Home of cousin Edward Gelles (b.1927), the published genealogist of our maternal Griffel-Nadwórna dynasty.

era_context.15th_century: Free imperial city of Mainz, Holy Roman Empire — original home of R. Meshullam Yekutiel ha-Kohen Rapa, progenitor of the Rapoport dynasty, before he fled to Italy.

places.note_en: Original home of the Rapoport dynasty progenitor in the 15th century, before the family fled to Italy.

era_context.15th_century: Jewish refugee community in Italy. R. Meshullam Yekutiel ha-Kohen Rapa (d.1450) settled here from Mainz. His descendants added 'Porto' to their surname — hence Rapa-Porto / Rapoport.

places.note_en: Where the Rapoport family takes its name. The dynasty's founder settled here after fleeing Mainz.

era_context.13th_14th_century: Kingdom of Mallorca — independent Catalan-speaking kingdom. Major medieval Jewish community including the Rapapa physicians (Dr Vidal 1305, Dr Jucef Salomon 1311). After 1391 pogroms and 1435 forced conversions, Mallorcan Jews became 'Chuetas'.
era_context.now: Capital of the Balearic Islands, Spain

places.note_en: Birthplace of the documented medieval Rapapa ancestors. The 1305 conspiracy to rescue a Jewish girl from forced conversion-by-marriage was led here by Dr Vidal Rapapa. Court physician Dr Jucef Salomon Rapapa served King Jaime III here. DNA testing confirmed living Mallorcan Chueta family ↔ US Rappoport family direct mitochondrial link.

era_context.16th_century: Renaissance Italian port on the Livaza river, Veneto region. Refuge for Jewish exiles. Birthplace of R. Yechiel Michael ha-Kohen Rapa (b.1502).
era_context.now: Comune in the Province of Treviso, Veneto, Italy

places.note_en: Where R. Yechiel Michael ha-Kohen Rapa was born in 1502. His son Isaac 'HaMoel' was the first to use the surname 'Rapa-Porto' (~1550), creating the modern Rapaport name.

era_context.16th_century: Major Italian Jewish printing centre. Rabbi Avraham Menachem ha-Kohen Rapa-Porto (1520-1596) published 'Mincha Belulah' here in 1594 with the iconic raven + priestly blessing family emblem.

places.note_en: Where R. Avraham Menachem ha-Kohen Rapa-Porto published Mincha Belulah in 1594, introducing the raven + priestly blessing family emblem still used by descendants today.

era_context.15th_16th_century: Major Renaissance Italian Jewish centre. Birthplace of R. Meir Katzenellenbogen 'MaHaRaM Padua' (1482-1565) and his son R. Samuel Judah Katzenellenbogen (1521-1597), founders of the Katzenellenbogen rabbinical dynasty that ultimately reached our family via Chawa Wahl.

places.note_en: Where the Katzenellenbogen rabbinical dynasty was founded. MaHaRaM Padua served as Chief Rabbi of Padua until 1565.

era_context.16th_17th_century: Major Polish-Lithuanian Jewish centre. Saul Wahl Katzenellenbogen and his son R. Meir Wahl 'MaHaRaSH' lived here.

places.note_en: Home of Saul Wahl Katzenellenbogen (the legendary 'King for one night' of Poland) and his rabbinical descendants.

era_context.19th_century: Galician Jewish town. Birthplace of Shulim Wahl and Sarah Safier — Chawa Wahl Griffel's parents.

places.note_en: Where Chawa Wahl (1877-1941), David Mendel Griffel's wife, was born — into the Tarnobrzeg Wahl line documented as descending from Saul Wahl Katzenellenbogen.

era_context.1918_1939: Regional capital of Stanisławów Voivodeship (containing Nadwórna).

places.note_en: Birthplace of Dr Chanan Rapaport (1928-2022), the Rapaport family genealogist. Regional capital of the voivodeship containing Nadwórna.

era_context.19th_century: Founding seat of the Kosov Hasidic dynasty.

places.note_en: Founding seat of the Hager Kosov-Vizhnitz Hasidic dynasty. Birthplace of Reb Chaim Hager of Ottynia (1863-1931).

era_context.19th_20th_century: Seat of the Ottynia branch of the Hager Hasidic dynasty.

places.note_en: Where Reb Chaim Hager (1863-1931) was rebbe. Eliezer Griffel and Berisz Rapaport prayed at the Ottynia kloiz.

era_context.19th_20th_century: Carpathian foothills town near Nadwórna. Birthplace of Mollie (Malke) Mehrbaum (b.~1890), who married Bernard Griffel of Nadwórna.

places.note_en: Birthplace of Mollie Mehrbaum, an in-law of the Nadwórna Griffel family.

era_context.1909: Town in eastern Galicia, Austria-Hungary — Carpathian foothills, ~60km from Bolechów + ~80km from Stryj.
era_context.1918_1939: Stryj district, Stanyslaviv Voivodeship, Second Polish Republic

places.note_en: Birthplace of S. Goldfischer (Dalia's father; Dana, Doron and Daniel's maternal grandfather, b.23 November 1909). Skole is in the same Galician Carpathian region as Bolechów, Stryj, Dolina + Nadwórna — connecting Dana, Doron and Daniel's paternal (Rapaport-Weitzner) and maternal (Goldfischer) lines to the same small Jewish world of eastern Galicia.

era_context.1947: Allied-occupied France post-war. Mediterranean fishing port used by Mossad LeAliyah Bet as a clandestine departure point for survivor-bearing ships to British Mandate Palestine.

places.significance: Departure port of the Aliyah Bet ship Theodor Herzl on 2 April 1947 with 2,641 ma'apilim — including David, Lusia, Shimon and infant Dov Rapaport.
places.significance_he: נמל היציאה של אוניית העלייה ב' תיאודור הרצל ב-2 באפריל 1947 עם 2,641 מעפילים — ובהם דוד, לוסיה, שמעון ודב התינוק.
places.significance_pl: Port wyjścia statku Aliyah Bet Theodor Herzl 2 kwietnia 1947 z 2 641 ma'apilim — w tym Dawid, Lusia, Szymon i niemowlę Dov.
places.significance_fr: Port de départ du navire Aliyah Bet Theodor Herzl le 2 avril 1947 avec 2 641 ma'apilim — dont David, Lusia, Szymon et le bébé Dov.

era_context.1946_1949: British-controlled Cyprus. Site of British internment camps for Jewish Holocaust survivors intercepted en route to British Mandate Palestine. 53,510 Jews held, ~2,200 children born in captivity.

places.significance: Internment site of David, Lusia, Shimon and infant Dov for ~8 months after the British boarded the Theodor Herzl in April 1947. Summer tent camps 55, 60, 61, 62, 63 were at Karaolos north of Famagusta; winter Nissen-hut camps were at Dekhelia near Larnaca.
places.significance_he: אתר המעצר של דוד, לוסיה, שמעון ודב התינוק במשך כ-8 חודשים לאחר השתלטות הבריטית על תיאודור הרצל באפריל 1947. מחנות אוהלים קיציים 55, 60, 61, 62, 63 היו בקראולוס צפונית לפמגוסטה; מחנות חורף עם צריפי ניסן היו בדכליה ליד לרנקה.
places.significance_pl: Miejsce internowania Dawida, Lusi, Szymona i niemowlęcia Dova przez około 8 miesięcy po przejęciu Theodora Herzla przez Brytyjczyków w kwietniu 1947. Letnie obozy namiotowe nr 55, 60, 61, 62, 63 znajdowały się w Karaolos koło Famagusty.
places.significance_fr: Lieu d'internement de David, Lusia, Szymon et Dov bébé pendant environ 8 mois après l'arraisonnement du Theodor Herzl en avril 1947. Camps d'été sous tente nos 55, 60, 61, 62, 63 à Karaolos, près de Famagouste.

era_context.1939_1948: British detention camp on the Mediterranean coast south of Haifa. Held intercepted Ma'apilim (Aliyah Bet illegal immigrants), unaccompanied minors and the wounded who could not be deported to Cyprus.

places.significance: Detention site of young Shimon Rapaport (David and Lusia's elder son) per his release document referenced in the memoir (p.65) — likely separated from his parents with the wounded from the Theodor Herzl and held at Atlit while David and Lusia were in Cyprus.
places.significance_he: אתר המעצר של שמעון רפפורט הצעיר (בכורם של דוד ולוסיה) לפי תעודת השחרור שלו המוזכרת ביומן (עמ' 65) — ככל הנראה הופרד מהוריו עם פצועי תיאודור הרצל בעוד דוד ולוסיה היו בקפריסין.
places.significance_pl: Miejsce zatrzymania młodego Szymona Rapaporta (starszego syna Dawida i Lusi) wg dokumentu zwolnienia wspomnianego w pamiętniku (s. 65).
places.significance_fr: Lieu de détention du jeune Szymon Rapaport (fils aîné de David et Lusia) selon son document de libération mentionné dans le mémoire (p. 65).

era_context.1947_1948: Main British-administered port of Mandate Palestine. Where Aliyah Bet ships were intercepted and where the State of Israel proclaimed independence in May 1948.
era_context.1948_: Major Israeli port city on Mount Carmel. The Rapaport family settled here after release from Cyprus.

places.significance: Arrival and lifelong-home city of the Rapaport family after Cyprus. David died here 29 August 1990, age 78, buried at Sde Yehoshua cemetery. Lusia died 28 December 1996, age 83, adjacent grave 102ג. Doron, Dana and Daniel grew up here.
places.significance_he: עיר ההגעה והבית של משפחת רפפורט לאחר קפריסין. דוד נפטר כאן ב-29 באוגוסט 1990, בן 78, נטמן בבית הקברות שדה יהושע. לוסיה נפטרה ב-28 בדצמבר 1996, בת 83, בקבר סמוך 102ג. דורון, דנה ודניאל גדלו כאן.
places.significance_pl: Miasto przybycia i pożycia rodziny Rapaport po Cyprze. Dawid zmarł tu 29 sierpnia 1990, wiek 78. Lusia zmarła 28 grudnia 1996, wiek 83.
places.significance_fr: Ville d'arrivée et de vie de la famille Rapaport après Chypre. David y mourut le 29 août 1990, à 78 ans. Lusia mourut le 28 décembre 1996, à 83 ans.

era_context.1946_1947: Major Mediterranean port in post-war Allied France. Used by Mossad LeAliyah Bet to refit acquired ships for the clandestine immigration to Palestine.

places.significance: Where the ex-British cable ship HMS Guardian (built 1907, Newcastle) was refitted in 1946-1947 for ~2,600 passengers before its rechristening as the 'Theodor Herzl' and the April 1947 voyage from Sète that carried David, Lusia, Shimon and infant Dov.
places.significance_he: המקום שבו שופצה ב-1946-1947 ספינת הכבלים הבריטית HMS גארדיאן (נבנתה ב-1907 בניוקאסל) להובלת כ-2,600 מעפילים, לפני קבלת השם 'תיאודור הרצל' והפלגתה מסט באפריל 1947.
places.significance_pl: Miejsce, gdzie w latach 1946-1947 brytyjski statek kablowy HMS Guardian został przebudowany na transport około 2 600 ma'apilim przed przemianowaniem na 'Theodor Herzl'.
places.significance_fr: Lieu où le câblier britannique HMS Guardian (construit en 1907 à Newcastle) fut réaménagé en 1946-1947 pour environ 2 600 passagers avant d'être rebaptisé « Theodor Herzl ».
